title: Golden Brown Moroccan Onion Tagine
description: A briny and aerated Moroccan dish featuring onions and shio-cure, perfect for a hangover cure, and ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly. This tagine is a flavorful and aromatic stew.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large clay or ceramic tagine or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the sliced onions and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are golden brown and caramelized.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Stir in the shio-cure, cumin, coriander, turmeric, salt, and black pepper.
Add 2 cups of water to the tagine and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro.
Refrigerate or freeze any leftovers for up to 3 days.
Reheat the tagine over low heat, stirring occasionally, until warmed through.
